name: Antipasto
recipeIngredient: Canned Italian tuna in olive oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Stock your shelves with these ingredients: in the cupboard, canned Italian tuna in olive oil, canned anchovy fillets, canned sardines, imported green and black olives, jarred pickled vegetables, dried dates, prunes, capers, breadsticks, extra virgin olive oil, dried tomatoes, wine, and balsamic vinegars. In the refrigerator, include celery, carrots, lemons, Parmigiano-Reggiano, provolone. In the freezer, keep roasted peppers and Italian bread.
2. Assemble a simple antipasto platter using jarred pickled vegetables, canned tuna, firm sharp cheese, olives, and breadsticks.
3. For additional ideas:
- Pitted dates slit and stuffed with small pieces of Parmigiano-Reggiano.
- Thin slices of prosciutto, ham, or salami wrapped around thin bread sticks, cooked asparagus spears, wedges of pears or persimmon.
- Thin slices of smoked salmon wrapped around thin bread sticks.
- Dried tomatoes soaked in warm water and drained, then pureed with garlic, olive oil, basil, and balsamic vinegar spread on toast or crackers.
- Raw vegetables like fennel, Belgian endive, carrots, celery cut into spears to dip in olive oil seasoned with coarse salt and freshly cracked pepper.
- Bite-sized wedges of Parmigiano-Reggiano seasoned with a drop of balsamic vinegar.
- Fresh goat cheese drizzled with olive oil, chopped garlic, crushed red pepper, oregano, parsley, or basil.
- Balls or cubes of fresh mozzarella tossed with olive oil, chopped anchovies, parsley, and crushed red pepper.
- Thin slices of leftover focaccia or Italian loaf brushed with olive oil and herbs baked until crisp.
